mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth consistently leads to negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by >0.5pp over 3 months, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and housing starts are simultaneously declining, it indicates tight monetary conditions severely impacting interest-rate sensitive sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the specified FEDFUNDS and HOUST conditions are met, but SPX 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low GDP growth, any positive acceleration in GDP signals a potential economic recovery or re-acceleration, which improves investor sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate accelerates by > 0.5 percentage points quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: If GDPC1 acceleration occurs during slow growth, but 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market participants react to inflation data releases.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 5.0%, but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of the subsequent periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market, strong consumer confidence, and robust economic activity, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently leads to negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding and retail sales are also growing, it signifies a healthy balance between supply and demand, indicating robust economic expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If both INDPRO and RSAFS growth conditions are met, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in over 40% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, any further increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals continued monetary tightening, which negatively impacts corporate borrowing costs and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases while already above 3.0%, but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of the subsequent periods over any 2-year rolling window.
